```json
{
  "risk": "HIGH",
  "riskReason": "Security vulnerability: unauthenticated negative offset pagination bypass allows unauthorized access to audit log data beyond normal pagination bounds.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "critical",
      "category": "Security: Authorization Bypass",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/paginator.py",
      "line": 868,
      "title": "Negative offset pagination allows unauthorized data access",
      "description": "The OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result() method at line 868 accepts negative offsets when `enable_advanced_features=True`, allowing clients to bypass pagination bounds and access arbitrary audit log records. The comment claims 'permissions are checked at the queryset level' but the audit log queryset is already built and filtered before pagination occurs. A malicious user with the `optimized_pagination` query parameter can craft negative cursor offsets to retrieve data outside their authorized range.",
      "suggestion": "Remove negative offset support entirely. If reverse pagination is needed, implement it through explicit cursor direction flags rather than offset manipulation. Ensure that pagination cannot access records outside the filtered queryset scope."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Security: Privilege Escalation",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py",
      "line": 72,
      "title": "enable_advanced_features flag trusts unvalidated user permission check",
      "description": "The condition `organization_context.member.has_global_access` is used to grant advanced pagination features, but this check occurs in the endpoint before paginator instantiation. A user could modify their request or exploit a race condition where membership changes between the permission check and paginator initialization, gaining unauthorized access to the negative offset bypass.",
      "suggestion": "Pass permission checks as immutable values to the paginator constructor rather than storing mutable state. Better yet, remove the `enable_advanced_features` flag entirely and disable negative offset support by default."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Logic Error: Boundary Violation",
      "file": "src/sentry/api/paginator.py",
      "line": 861,
      "title": "Negative offset slice bypasses queryset filtering entirely",
      "description": "When `enable_advanced_features=True` and `cursor.offset < 0`, the code executes `queryset[start_offset:stop]` with a negative start_offset (line 861). Django querysets do support negative indexing in Python evaluation, but this is evaluated lazily and can access records that should have been filtered out by the base queryset's permission checks. The audit log endpoint builds the queryset with user-specific filters, then the paginator ignores those bounds.",
      "suggestion": "Remove the negative offset branch entirely (lines 864-866). Enforce `start_offset = max(0, offset)` unconditionally as the safe default."
    }
  ]
}
```
